name: xAI
headline: xAI FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — no new xAI-specific conduct event on May 16. Carry-forward from May 14-15: (1) three-failure governance pattern (CSAM/NCII scale, Musk-protection prompt manipulation, May 14 political-bias prompt modification); (2) Pentagon classified access without restrictions (Axios Feb 23; CNBC March 26); (3) May 14 partial-external-accountability-reversal (GitHub system-prompt-publication pledge under same-day operational pressure). Grok CSAM incident continued international regulatory attention per May 16 search results: 8+ countries confirmed regulatory action; 3M+ sexualized images in two weeks including 23,000+ child-apparent images (CCDH confirmed); California C&D, NY AG, Michigan AG all active. Global AI safety governance publications (Just Security, National Law Review, Geopolitical Monitor) cite Grok as primary example of ungoverned AI. Continued international amplification strengthens first-baseline evidence quality without adding new facts. Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. Carry-over from May 14-15 three-category event package.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. Carry-over. No EMP-dimension uplift evidence in window.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. Carry-over. May 14 GitHub pledge remains forward-looking promise; no execution evidence in window.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Carry-over. CSAM/NCII victims + political minorities differential-harm pattern continues.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. Carry-over. Individual-employee production-prompt modification authority remains active BND-dimension governance gap.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. Carry-over. May 14 partial-external-accountability-reversal sub-anchor signal carried forward; does not move dimension off floor. International amplification (8+ countries, multi-AG, CCDH) strengthens external accountability invocation density without producing actor self-accountability.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. Pentagon-classified-access-without-restrictions pattern is SYS-dimension sector signal; xAI commercial posture in military contracting is structured around absence of safety-governance commitments.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. Sector contrast (xAI gained Pentagon access by accepting no restrictions; Anthropic lost it by maintaining limits) is INT-dimension sector-relative signal confirming xAI's posture choice as commercially-incentivized rather than values-aligned.

evidence.summary: Just Security: 'What ungoverned AI looks like' — Grok cited as primary example.
AICerts.ai: xAI/Grok safety failure spurs global CSAM lawsuits; 8+ countries confirmed regulatory action.
AI Automation Global: xAI Grok Pentagon classified AI safety divide 2026 — perverse-procurement-incentive sector pattern.
NY AG James demanded more action xAI to stop Grok chatbot producing problematic content.

keyEvidence: Continued international amplification: 8+ countries, multi-AG, CCDH primary data.
Pentagon classified access without restrictions vs Anthropic exclusion for maintaining limits — sector INT signal.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. The continued international amplification of the CSAM governance failure strengthens the first-baseline evidence quality but doesn't add new facts. The 'perverse-procurement-incentive' sector pattern (xAI gained Pentagon access by accepting no restrictions while Anthropic lost it by maintaining limits) remains the most methodologically novel sector signal for the AI labs cluster. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: Multi-AG regulatory action: California C&D, NY AG, Michigan AG follow-up
Pentagon classified-AI cohort: any restriction-pledge updates
Musk v. Altman trial: any spillover to xAI
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Floor sustained at 0.0.
